learning_outcomes.description: Reflect on and evaluate your employability skillset and attributes.
Communicate your employability skillset and attributes.
Create a professional development plan to further build upon your employability skills.
Critically reflect on and articulate key business practices, processes and challenges within an organisation.
Demonstrate the ability to think strategically from the perspective of a manager.
Explain your industry employment requirements and trends.
Clarify your career goals and pathways.

assessments.due_date:
A2.1 Written reflection 31/07/2026 5:00 pm

A2.2 Interactive oral 21/08/2026 5:00 pm

For the A2.2 Interactive Oral Assessment, students are required to schedule an interview time with the lecturer.
assessments.task_description: To complete this assessment and pass the course, you must secure a host organisation that can provide meaningful work to support skill development, arrange and complete the placement through an agreed schedule (e.g., block, part-time weekly) that will total 80-100 hours, negotiate working hours with the organisation and have the internship supervisor (or appropriate designate) complete the industry supervisor evaluation form.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The industry supervisor evaluation form will be submitted online by your supervisor who would have received a link to the form at the time your internship was approved. Once they’ve completed and submitted the form you will automatically receive an emailed copy.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.due_date:
A2.1 Written reflection 31/07/2026 5:00 pm

A2.2 Interactive oral 21/08/2026 5:00 pm

For the A2.2 Interactive Oral Assessment, students are required to schedule an interview time with the lecturer.
assessments.task_description:
In this assessment, you will showcase your reflective skills by analysing and reflecting on the business/organisation you observed and engaged with during your placement. You will critically reflect on the practices, processes, challenges and/or successes of the business or organisation you worked in. Your observations will lead you to develop a foundational understanding of how businesses operate, their managerial decision-making, and how businesses respond to wider industry/economic forces.

The assessment will consist of two parts:

  • A2.1: A written reflection that presents your observations and critical analysis of a significant/consequential practice, process, challenge, and/or opportunity within the business or organisation in which you worked. The reflection should also explore the key learnings gained from this experience and explicitly link these insights to the development of your skills and personal attributes.
  • A2.2: A semi-structured in-person consultation with your lecturer during which you will discuss the issue you identified in A2.1, as well as the learnings you gained with reference to your employability skillset and attributes.

AI statement

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: A2.1: Submitted by the due date via the assessment submission link on the course's learn.uq site (Blackboard).
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
In this assessment you will write a comprehensive and self-reflective Professional Development Plan. You will evaluate your employability in the context of:

  • self-evaluation of your placement performance,
  • feedback you receive from supervisors/mentors/colleagues,
  • various self-test instruments,
  • research into job/industry requirements and trends, as well as
  • UQ’s graduate attributes.

You will identify specific areas for improvement (development areas), and develop a clear action plan to achieve your development goals. The assessment concludes with a strategic perspective of your professional identity and long-term career aspirations. You will consider career pathways that align with your goals for both personal and professional growth.

AI statement

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

learning_activities.topic:
Industry placement with a host organisation

80-100 hours placement with an industry host. The placement will give you the opportunity to gain valuable work experience and to connect this experience to the development of a workplace ready skill set.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Industry placement with a host organisation 80-100 hours placement with an industry host. The placement will give you the opportunity to gain valuable work experience and to connect this experience to the development of a workplace ready skill set.

learning_activities.topic:
Employ101X - MOOC Unlocking your employability

This is an optional self-paced online course in which you will hear from:

  • Employers discussing what they value when hiring new graduates
  • Current students who have maximised their university experience
  • Graduates who have transitioned successfully into the workplace
  • Academics in the field of employability with insights into key topics in the course

Learning outcomes: L01
